title: Ithaca's king defies the gods in final The Odyssey trailer
gist: Universal released a final trailer for Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey, an all-star Matt Damon-led adaptation shot entirely in IMAX and releasing July 17, 2026 in IMAX plus 70mm and 35mm formats.

insights.claim: Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey, distributed by Universal Pictures, releases July 17, 2026, was shot entirely in IMAX, and will screen in IMAX plus 70mm and 35mm film formats.
insights.speaker: Jennifer Ouellette
insights.so_what: A full-IMAX capture and premium large-format rollout signals Universal betting on theatrical/premium-format economics and IMAX screen availability for a summer tentpole.
insights.quote: The film was shot entirely in IMAX; it will release in IMAX and on 75 and 35 mm.
insights.locator: The Odyssey hits theaters on July 17, 2026

insights.claim: Universal ran a sustained multi-stage marketing campaign for The Odyssey, releasing first stills in February 2025, a teaser in July, a full trailer in December, a second trailer in May, and a final trailer, with the final trailer largely reusing prior footage.
insights.speaker: Jennifer Ouellette
insights.so_what: A ~17-month drip campaign shows the marketing cadence studios use to sustain anticipation for a major auteur release.
insights.locator: We got our first look at stills

insights.claim: The Odyssey assembles an unusually deep star cast including Matt Damon, Anne Hathaway, Tom Holland, Robert Pattinson, Zendaya, Charlize Theron, and Lupita Nyong'o in a dual role, concentrating marquee talent in a single Nolan production.
insights.speaker: Jennifer Ouellette
insights.so_what: Concentrated A-list casting is Universal's principal draw and cost driver for the film's box-office thesis.
insights.locator: Matt Damon stars as the wandering Ithacan king
